title="Levels of Care"> Levels of Care</strong></li></ul></div> <div class="page-info"> <h1 class="page-title">Levels of Care</h1> <p class="page-intro"></p> </div> </div> <section class="top-text-callout"> <p itemprop="description">BrightQuest provides compassionate care within a therapeutic community where individuals are given the opportunity to move through progressive phases of treatment in a supportive, inclusive atmosphere. Each level of care is designed to meet our clients where they are in their journey so they can develop sustainable daily living skills and achieve greater independence. </p> </section> <section> <div class="quick-links custom-sidebar-background"> <h3 class="quicklinks-heading custom-sidebar-title">Page Contents</h3> <hr class="float-left full-width" /> <ul> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#5-residential-treatment-center-rtc-1">Residential Treatment Center - RTC-1</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#6-residential-partial-hospitalization-program-prep-rtc-2-php-prep-">Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program Prep - RTC-2 (PHP-Prep)</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#7-partial-hospitalization-program-php-with-semi-independent-housing">Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#8-iop-with-semi-independent-housing">IOP with Semi-Independent Housing</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#9-outpatient">Outpatient</a></li> </ul> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <div class="section-content"> <p>At BrightQuest, each individual is supported in developing and practicing skills that help them live a more independent and fulfilling life. Members of the community are empowered to make choices, search for options, and solve problems effectively. Self-esteem is strengthened and the development of life skills are gained through the process of discovery and learning. The primary goal at BrightQuest is to help our clients learn and integrate the skills necessary to live a happy, healthy, and independent life.</p> </div> </div> </section> <section class="video"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <div class="embed-container"> <iframe title="2025 BrightQuest Levels of Care Explained" width="640" height="360" frameborder="0" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share;" nitro-og-src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/MGdKJ16hNj4?feature=oembed&autoplay=1" nitro-lazy-src="data:text/html;https://www.youtube.com/embed/MGdKJ16hNj4?feature=oembed&autoplay=1;base64," class="nitro-lazy"></iframe> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="5-residential-treatment-center-rtc-1" class="section-title">Residential Treatment Center - RTC-1</h2> <hr class="nitro-lazy" /> <div class="section-content"> <p>Our Residential Treatment Center is a non-locked, licensed residential treatment center that provides 24-hour supported and monitored living. This is our highest level of care and features:</p> <ul> <li>Private or shared rooms in our state licensed adult residential treatment facility with 24/7 onsite staff</li> <li>A focus on stabilization of mental health symptoms and establishing fundamental daily structure</li> <li>Clinical programming delivered in the residence with a focus on health and safety, medication compliance, social interaction, and basic activities of daily living</li> <li>Nursing onsite 8 hours per day</li> <li>Meals planned and prepared by onsite Nutrition Aide</li> <li>Medication management and transportation needs are staff facilitated</li> <li>Family therapy centers on psychoeducation, setting goals, and stabilizing patterns</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="6-residential-partial-hospitalization-program-prep-rtc-2-php-prep-" class="section-title">Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program Prep - RTC-2 (PHP-Prep)</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <p>Our Residential PHP Prep homes are non-locked, licensed residential treatment facilities that provide 24-hour staff support while clients are in the home. Clients in this level of care are beginning to take on more autonomy, but still benefit from prompting and reminders from staff and others to engage in coping skills and basic activities of daily living. This level of care also features:</p> <ul> <li>Private or shared rooms in our state licensed adult supported living residence with 24/7 onsite staff</li> <li>A bridge between residential and PHP with housing focused on the skills necessary for living without onsite staff support such as medication adherence, healthy self-care, socialization, and organization</li> <li>Clinical programming occurs both in the residence and at the BrightQuest offices with an emphasis on vocational/educational assessment and goal setting, establishing social support networks, and developing effective behavioral strategies for self-management</li> <li>Meals planned and prepared by onsite Nutrition Aide</li> <li>Nursing onsite 8 hours per day</li> <li>Medication management and transportation needs are staff-facilitated</li> <li>Family therapy centers on history, communication, and healthy coping strategies</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="7-partial-hospitalization-program-php-with-semi-independent-housing" class="section-title">Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <p>PHP with Semi-Independent Housing may be the recommended entry point to BrightQuest for some of our new clients. Though no overnight, live-in house manager is present; BrightQuest staff regularly visits each house in addition to visits each morning and evening, 7 days per week, to verify medication compliance, chore completion, and to confirm that the house is in good order. This level of care also features:</p> <ul> <li>Private or shared rooms in one of our semi-independent living homes</li> <li>A design that supports the development of advanced independence such as time management, budgeting, socializing, shopping, and meal preparation</li> <li>Clinical programming occurs at the BrightQuest offices with a focus on proficiency and the sustained practice of safe and healthy self-care and symptom management, and complex interpersonal skills</li> <li>Medication management and transportation needs are staff supported</li> <li>Family therapy concentrates on maintaining skills such as setting and holding limits and boundaries, and open communication. Family therapy includes exploring family roles and relational dynamics</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="8-iop-with-semi-independent-housing" class="section-title">IOP with Semi-Independent Housing</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <p>Our IOP homes are residential homes located in the surrounding community. BrightQuest staff regularly visit each house throughout the day to verify medication compliance, chore completion, and to confirm that the house is in good order. Clients in this level of care have begun to consistently take personal responsibility and initiative in managing the various domains of their lives. Clients begin tapering down clinical programming, while beginning the transition to independent living. This level of care also features:</p> <ul> <li>Private or shared rooms in one of our semi-independent living homes</li> <li>A design that focuses on increased independence. Clients begin tapering down clinical programming, while beginning the transition to independent living</li> <li>Clinical programming occurs at the BrightQuest offices with a focus on increased independence and preparing for entry or return to work or school</li> <li>Medication management and transportation are either staff-supported, peer-supported or self-supported depending on individual need</li> <li>Clients receive individual Work Development assignments for volunteering, work, or school. Individual meetings with a Work Development Coordinator are established to build on vocational work including starting school or obtaining a job</li> <li>Family therapy concentrates on ongoing maintenance of skills and adjusted familial roles with minimal clinical prompting and planning for the transition into independent living</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="9-outpatient" class="section-title">Outpatient</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <p>This is for clients who have completed our IOP. In the Extended Services level of care, clients live independently. This level of care offers BrightQuest clients an individualized therapeutic structure to continue their progress. This allows clients to keep up with daily activities and responsibilities while receiving the clinical support needed to maintain stability and independence.</p> <ul> <li>A la carte outpatient therapy services are available for those who have successfully completed previous levels of care and have achieved a level of independence commensurate with living without staff support or supervision.</li> <li>All medical, psychiatric, and financial needs are managed by the client and their family with minimal program oversight or case management.</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> </div> <section BrightQuest provides compassionate care within a therapeutic community where individuals are given the opportunity to move through progressive phases of treatment in a supportive, inclusive atmosphere. Each level of care is designed to meet our clients where they are in their journey so they can develop sustainable daily living skills and achieve greater independence. 
 
 
 
 
Page Contents
 
 Residential Treatment Center - RTC-1 
 
 Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program Prep - RTC-2 (PHP-Prep) 
 
 Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing 
 
 IOP with Semi-Independent Housing 
 
 Outpatient 
 
 
 
 
 
 
At BrightQuest, each individual is supported in developing and practicing skills that help them live a more independent and fulfilling life. Members of the community are empowered to make choices, search for options, and solve problems effectively. Self-esteem is strengthened and the development of life skills are gained through the process of discovery and learning. The primary goal at BrightQuest is to help our clients learn and integrate the skills necessary to live a happy, healthy, and independent life.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Residential Treatment Center - RTC-1
 
 
Our Residential Treatment Center is a non-locked, licensed residential treatment center that provides 24-hour supported and monitored living. This is our highest level of care and features:
 
Private or shared rooms in our state licensed adult residential treatment facility with 24/7 onsite staff
 
A focus on stabilization of mental health symptoms and establishing fundamental daily structure
 
Clinical programming delivered in the residence with a focus on health and safety, medication compliance, social interaction, and basic activities of daily living
 
Nursing onsite 8 hours per day
 
Meals planned and prepared by onsite Nutrition Aide
 
Medication management and transportation needs are staff facilitated
 
Family therapy centers on psychoeducation, setting goals, and stabilizing patterns
 
 
 
 
 
 
Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program Prep - RTC-2 (PHP-Prep)
 
 
Our Residential PHP Prep homes are non-locked, licensed residential treatment facilities that provide 24-hour staff support while clients are in the home. Clients in this level of care are beginning to take on more autonomy, but still benefit from prompting and reminders from staff and others to engage in coping skills and basic activities of daily living. This level of care also features:
 
Private or shared rooms in our state licensed adult supported living residence with 24/7 onsite staff
 
A bridge between residential and PHP with housing focused on the skills necessary for living without onsite staff support such as medication adherence, healthy self-care, socialization, and organization
 
Clinical programming occurs both in the residence and at the BrightQuest offices with an emphasis on vocational/educational assessment and goal setting, establishing social support networks, and developing effective behavioral strategies for self-management
 
Meals planned and prepared by onsite Nutrition Aide
 
Nursing onsite 8 hours per day
 
Medication management and transportation needs are staff-facilitated
 
Family therapy centers on history, communication, and healthy coping strategies
 
 
 
 
 
 
Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing
 
 
PHP with Semi-Independent Housing may be the recommended entry point to BrightQuest for some of our new clients. Though no overnight, live-in house manager is present; BrightQuest staff regularly visits each house in addition to visits each morning and evening, 7 days per week, to verify medication compliance, chore completion, and to confirm that the house is in good order. This level of care also features:
 
Private or shared rooms in one of our semi-independent living homes
 
A design that supports the development of advanced independence such as time management, budgeting, socializing, shopping, and meal preparation
 
Clinical programming occurs at the BrightQuest offices with a focus on proficiency and the sustained practice of safe and healthy self-care and symptom management, and complex interpersonal skills
 
Medication management and transportation needs are staff supported
 
Family therapy concentrates on maintaining skills such as setting and holding limits and boundaries, and open communication. Family therapy includes exploring family roles and relational dynamics
 
 
 
 
 
 
IOP with Semi-Independent Housing
 
 
Our IOP homes are residential homes located in the surrounding community. BrightQuest staff regularly visit each house throughout the day to verify medication compliance, chore completion, and to confirm that the house is in good order. Clients in this level of care have begun to consistently take personal responsibility and initiative in managing the various domains of their lives. Clients begin tapering down clinical programming, while beginning the transition to independent living. This level of care also features:
 
Private or shared rooms in one of our semi-independent living homes
 
A design that focuses on increased independence. Clients begin tapering down clinical programming, while beginning the transition to independent living
 
Clinical programming occurs at the BrightQuest offices with a focus on increased independence and preparing for entry or return to work or school
 
Medication management and transportation are either staff-supported, peer-supported or self-supported depending on individual need
 
Clients receive individual Work Development assignments for volunteering, work, or school. Individual meetings with a Work Development Coordinator are established to build on vocational work including starting school or obtaining a job
 
Family therapy concentrates on ongoing maintenance of skills and adjusted familial roles with minimal clinical prompting and planning for the transition into independent living
 
 
 
 
 
 
Outpatient
 
 
This is for clients who have completed our IOP. In the Extended Services level of care, clients live independently. This level of care offers BrightQuest clients an individualized therapeutic structure to continue their progress. This allows clients to keep up with daily activities and responsibilities while receiving the clinical support needed to maintain stability and independence.
 
A la carte outpatient therapy services are available for those who have successfully completed previous levels of care and have achieved a level of independence commensurate with living without staff support or supervision.
 
All medical, psychiatric, and financial needs are managed by the client and their family with minimal program oversight or case management.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Help is only a phone call away.
 
 
 619-466-0547 
 
 
 
 
Our Levels of Care

Take the first step. We're here to help.
 
 Don’t wait another day to get the help you or a loved one needs.
 Call for a free confidential assessment.

We are here to listen compassionately. Our free, confidential telephone consultation will help you find treatment that will work for you, whether it is with us or a different program.
 We can guide you in approaching a loved one who needs treatment.
 
We're here to listen

id="breadcrumbs" class="breadcrumbs" itemprop="breadcrumb"><li class="item-home"><a class="bread-link bread-home" href="https://www.brightquest.com" title="Homepage"><span class="ion-ios-home-outline separator-1873"> </li><li class="item-current item-1879"><strong title="Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Semi-Independent Housing"> Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Semi-Independent Housing</strong></li></ul></div> <div class="page-info"> <h1 class="page-title">Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Housing</h1> <p class="page-intro"></p> </div> </div> <section class="top-text-callout"> <p itemprop="description">BrightQuest's clinical team determines the most appropriate level of care for each client during the assessment process. Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing is often the recommended entry point to BrightQuest clients. </p> </section> <section> <div class="quick-links custom-sidebar-background"> <h3 class="quicklinks-heading custom-sidebar-title">Page Contents</h3> <hr class="float-left full-width" /> <ul> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#4-learning-and-practicing-greater-independence">Learning and Practicing Greater Independence</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#5-vocational-educational-support">Vocational & Educational Support</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#6-socialization-activity-involvement-builds-healthy-relationships">Socialization & Activity Involvement Builds Healthy Relationships</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#7-intensive-psychotherapy-for-emotional-balance-and-health">Intensive Psychotherapy for Emotional Balance and Health</a></li> <li class="custom-sidebar-content offset-line-height padding-bottom-xs"><a href="#8-staff-support-">Staff Support </a></li> </ul> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <div class="section-content"> <p>The PHP with Semi-Independent Housing level of care is often the recommended entry point to BrightQuest for many of our new clients.</p> <p>As a program designed to teach independence, we want our clients to start learning these skill as soon as possible. This means guiding clients through managing chores around the house, cooking meals together and buying groceries based on the agreed to meal plans.</p> <p>For clients needing support to complete these types of tasks, BrightQuest will provide a one-on-one support staff person whose job would be to help the client learn how to complete assigned tasks.</p> <p>Clients will spend much of their time at BrightQuest in this level of care. Once they have demonstrated the ability to appropriately manage these life skills, we start the process of moving them into our IOP with Housing level of care. IOP with Semi-Independent Housing builds on the skills learned during the PHP with Semi-Independent Housing level of care. </p> <p> </p> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="4-learning-and-practicing-greater-independence" class="section-title">Learning and Practicing Greater Independence</h2> <hr class="nitro-lazy" /> <div class="section-content"> <p><strong>The cornerstones of PHP with Semi-Independent Housing include:</strong></p> <ul> <li>A private or shared room in one of our <a href="https://www.brightquest.com/san-diego/setting/">semi-independent living homes.</a></li> <li>Household management training (meals, budgeting, chores, etc.)</li> <li>Guidance for medication management and compliance</li> <li>Support in developing effective daily self-care skills</li> <li>Learning to live cooperatively</li> </ul> <p> </p> <p><strong>IOP with Semi-Independent Housing builds on these skills and places an added emphasis on:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Transitioning to independent living</li> <li>Entering or returning to work or school</li> <li>Family therapy concentrates on maintaining skills and adjusted familial roles with minimal clinical prompting and planning for the transition into independent living</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="5-vocational-educational-support" class="section-title">Vocational & Educational Support</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <ul> <li>10 hours per week of volunteer work, paid employment or educational classes</li> <li>Job/school/training placement assistance</li> <li>Resume and interview preparation</li> <li>Support in work/school conflict resolution</li> <li>Development of a strong work ethic and commitment</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="6-socialization-activity-involvement-builds-healthy-relationships" class="section-title">Socialization & Activity Involvement Builds Healthy Relationships</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <ul> <li>9 hours per week of client-coordinated, peer-to-peer social activities</li> <li>Optional weekly staff supervised activities</li> <li>Bi-Monthly all-community outings (Beach BBQ’s, client/staff athletic events, etc)</li> <li>Holiday get-togethers <ul class="two_col"> <li>New Years</li> <li>Spring Celebration</li> <li>Memorial Day</li> <li>4th of July</li> <li>Labor Day</li> <li>Halloween</li> <li>Thanksgiving</li> <li>Holiday Celebration</li> </ul> </li> <li>Staff supervised weekly activity groups: <ul> <li>Board Games</li> <li>Saturday Morning Outing in the City</li> </ul> </li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="7-intensive-psychotherapy-for-emotional-balance-and-health" class="section-title">Intensive Psychotherapy for Emotional Balance and Health</h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <p>Group therapy program 5 days per week (up to 15 group therapy hours/week) including:</p> <ul> <li>Trauma and Recovery</li> <li>Bioenergetics</li> <li>Community Meeting</li> <li>Art Therapy</li> <li>Work Development</li> <li>Music Therapy</li> <li>Treatment Group</li> <li>Peer Support Group</li> <li>Process Group</li> <li>Social Skills Training</li> <li>Breaking Old Patterns</li> <li>Anger Management</li> <li>Cognitive (CBT) Skills</li> <li>Dialectical Behavioral (DBT) Skills</li> <li>BrightQuest Culture Group</li> <li>Improv/Drama Group</li> <li>“Eco” Group</li> <li>Up to three weekly individual therapy sessions</li> <li>Regular meetings with our program Psychiatrist (monthly or more frequent if indicated)</li> <li>Weekly family therapy</li> <li><a href="https://www.brightquest.com/our-program/family/">Bi-monthly Family Support Group events</a></li> <li>Clinical Coordination with additional care providers (hospitals, physicians, dentists, etc.)</li> </ul> </div> </div> </section> <section class="content-block"> <div class="padding-top-content"> <h2 id="8-staff-support-" class="section-title">Staff Support </h2> <hr /> <div class="section-content"> <p>Though no overnight, live-in manager is present at the PHP with Semi-Independent Housing and IOP with Semi-Independent Housing levels of care, BrightQuest staff regularly visits each throughout the day to verify medication compliance, chore completion and to confirm that the home is in good order. Any deviation from what we believe is appropriate and expected would be reported to the clinical team so the necessary therapeutic interventions can take place.</p> <p>Each client participates in a weekly house meeting facilitated by a BrightQuest House Manager.</p> <p>During this meeting chore rotations, cooking rotations and grocery shopping rotations are determined. In addition, the clients work together to plan meals for the upcoming week and also get support to resolve any conflicts that may need addressing.</p> </div> </div> </section> </div> <section  Our Program 

 

 Levels of Care 

 

 Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Semi-Independent Housing 
 
 
 
Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Housing
 

 
 
 
 
BrightQuest's clinical team determines the most appropriate level of care for each client during the assessment process. Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing is often the recommended entry point to BrightQuest clients. 
 
 
 
 
Page Contents
 
 Learning and Practicing Greater Independence 
 
 Vocational & Educational Support 
 
 Socialization & Activity Involvement Builds Healthy Relationships 
 
 Intensive Psychotherapy for Emotional Balance and Health 
 
 Staff Support 
 
 
 
 
 
 
The PHP with Semi-Independent Housing level of care is often the recommended entry point to BrightQuest for many of our new clients.
 
As a program designed to teach independence, we want our clients to start learning these skill as soon as possible. This means guiding clients through managing chores around the house, cooking meals together and buying groceries based on the agreed to meal plans.
 
For clients needing support to complete these types of tasks, BrightQuest will provide a one-on-one support staff person whose job would be to help the client learn how to complete assigned tasks.
 
Clients will spend much of their time at BrightQuest in this level of care. Once they have demonstrated the ability to appropriately manage these life skills, we start the process of moving them into our IOP with Housing level of care. IOP with Semi-Independent Housing builds on the skills learned during the PHP with Semi-Independent Housing level of care. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Learning and Practicing Greater Independence
 
 
 The cornerstones of PHP with Semi-Independent Housing include: 
 
A private or shared room in one of our semi-independent living homes. 
 
Household management training (meals, budgeting, chores, etc.)
 
Guidance for medication management and compliance
 
Support in developing effective daily self-care skills
 
Learning to live cooperatively
 
 
 
 IOP with Semi-Independent Housing builds on these skills and places an added emphasis on: 
 
Transitioning to independent living
 
Entering or returning to work or school
 
Family therapy concentrates on maintaining skills and adjusted familial roles with minimal clinical prompting and planning for the transition into independent living
 
 
 
 
 
 
Vocational & Educational Support
 
 
10 hours per week of volunteer work, paid employment or educational classes
 
Job/school/training placement assistance
 
Resume and interview preparation
 
Support in work/school conflict resolution
 
Development of a strong work ethic and commitment
 
 
 
 
 
 
Socialization & Activity Involvement Builds Healthy Relationships
 
 
9 hours per week of client-coordinated, peer-to-peer social activities
 
Optional weekly staff supervised activities
 
Bi-Monthly all-community outings (Beach BBQ’s, client/staff athletic events, etc)
 
Holiday get-togethers 
New Years
 
Spring Celebration
 
Memorial Day
 
4th of July
 
Labor Day
 
Halloween
 
Thanksgiving
 
Holiday Celebration
 
 
Staff supervised weekly activity groups: 
Board Games
 
Saturday Morning Outing in the City
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
Intensive Psychotherapy for Emotional Balance and Health
 
 
Group therapy program 5 days per week (up to 15 group therapy hours/week) including:
 
Trauma and Recovery
 
Bioenergetics
 
Community Meeting
 
Art Therapy
 
Work Development
 
Music Therapy
 
Treatment Group
 
Peer Support Group
 
Process Group
 
Social Skills Training
 
Breaking Old Patterns
 
Anger Management
 
Cognitive (CBT) Skills
 
Dialectical Behavioral (DBT) Skills
 
BrightQuest Culture Group
 
Improv/Drama Group
 
“Eco” Group
 
Up to three weekly individual therapy sessions
 
Regular meetings with our program Psychiatrist (monthly or more frequent if indicated)
 
Weekly family therapy
 
 Bi-monthly Family Support Group events 
 
Clinical Coordination with additional care providers (hospitals, physicians, dentists, etc.)
 
 
 
 
 
 
Staff Support 
 
 
Though no overnight, live-in manager is present at the PHP with Semi-Independent Housing and IOP with Semi-Independent Housing levels of care, BrightQuest staff regularly visits each throughout the day to verify medication compliance, chore completion and to confirm that the home is in good order. Any deviation from what we believe is appropriate and expected would be reported to the clinical team so the necessary therapeutic interventions can take place.
 
Each client participates in a weekly house meeting facilitated by a BrightQuest House Manager.
 
During this meeting chore rotations, cooking rotations and grocery shopping rotations are determined. In addition, the clients work together to plan meals for the upcoming week and also get support to resolve any conflicts that may need addressing.

Residential Treatment Centers for Adults with Complex Psychiatric Disorders
We provide a unique and effective combination of psychiatry, psychotherapy, and integrative therapy in a long-term residential setting.
Residential Treatment Center (RTC)
We are also in-network with Magellan Health for our residential level of care.
Please be aware that not all insurance policies include defined benefits for residential treatment.
BrightQuest: Residential Mental Health Treatment in San Diego, California
Transform Your Life at Nashville’s Premier Residential Mental Health Center
Outpatient Services Offer Support After Residential Treatment

It builds on the skills gained within the residential programs and is adapted to the needs of those living independently to sustain and build on the therapeutic foundation established in higher levels of care.
Cost-Effective Treatment: Our program offers affordable options to continue a therapeutic support structure after residential.

Continued Support: Our IOP and Outpatient Services offerings provide individuals the opportunity to extend their treatment as a means of maintaining the therapeutic improvements made during residential treatment as well as building upon those achievements.
While in residential treatment, our clients build strong connections with fellow peers and staff in a culture that promotes honesty, respect, and balance.
Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep

In this interview, he discusses what BrightQuest brings to the mental health landscape in Tennessee as well as the benefits of individualized, residential mental health treatment versus hospitalization .
And there is a big gray area between the acute and the office-based care where we don’t have good longer-term residential care options in this state.
BrightQuest Treatment Centers offer long-term residential treatment for people struggling with complex mental health disorders.
He has been involved in both inpatient and outpatient care, in private practice and in psychiatric hospitals around Nashville.
Residential Treatment for Mood Disorders – BrightQuest Treatment Centers
Residential Treatment for Mood Disorders
Residential treatment for mood disorders at BrightQuest has helped many recover their emotional equilibrium, restoring their ability to enjoy life and achieve their most cherished goals.
Residential Treatment for Mood Disorders at BrightQuest
How to Help a Loved One Get Residential Treatment for Mood Disorders

Residential treatment for mood disorders at BrightQuest offers an avenue of escape for those who are ready to fight back against conditions that have robbed them of their freedom to find happiness and choose their own destinies.
Residential treatment for mood disorders at BrightQuest offers our clients an ideal mix of comprehensive treatment services, in a personalized package that will address their unique individual needs.
Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep – BrightQuest Treatment Centers
Our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep homes are non-locked, licensed residential treatment facilities that provide 24-hour staff support.

The Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep is for clients who are beginning to take on more autonomy, but still require prompting and reminders from staff to engage in coping skills and basic activities of daily living.
In our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep level of care, residents can begin their journey of learning new life skills to put them on the path toward greater independence.
Our caring staff supports our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep clients in learning budgeting, grocery shopping, cooking, and more.

Clients in our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep level of care receive treatment and programming services both in their residence and at the BrightQuest Center, where they begin to interact with clients from other homes.
What makes our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep special is that it truly welcomes our clients into our therapeutic model of healing.
Residential Treatment Center - RTC-1
Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program Prep - RTC-2 (PHP-Prep)
Our Residential Treatment Center is a non-locked, licensed residential treatment center that provides 24-hour supported and monitored living.
Private or shared rooms in our state licensed adult residential treatment facility with 24/7 onsite staff
Our Residential PHP Prep homes are non-locked, licensed residential treatment facilities that provide 24-hour staff support while clients are in the home.

A bridge between residential and PHP with housing focused on the skills necessary for living without onsite staff support such as medication adherence, healthy self-care, socialization, and organization
Our IOP homes are residential homes located in the surrounding community.

Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient – BrightQuest Treatment Centers
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient Services

Led by a team of skilled professionals, our Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient Services options offer BrightQuest clients an individualized therapeutic structure after completing the BrightQuest Program.
Outpatient Services Offer Support After Residential Treatment
Benefits of Using our IOP and Outpatient Services
Clients in our IOP and Outpatient Program will receive exceptional treatment coordinated to work harmoniously with employment, school or other important commitments in their everyday lives.
We understand the vital role outpatient therapy can play in the treatment continuum and want to support you and your loved-one in receiving the necessary services for continued progress and success.
For more information about our IOP or Outpatient Services, contact our admission department at 619-466-0547.
Advantages of our IOP and Outpatient Services Program include:

Continued Support: Our IOP and Outpatient Services offerings provide individuals the opportunity to extend their treatment as a means of maintaining the therapeutic improvements made during residential treatment as well as building upon those achievements.
He has been involved in both inpatient and outpatient care, in private practice and in psychiatric hospitals around Nashville.
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) with Housing
Outpatient

A la carte outpatient therapy services are available for those who have successfully completed previous levels of care and have achieved a level of independence commensurate with living without staff support or supervision.
Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Housing

Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep
PHP with Housing
Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep – BrightQuest Treatment Centers
Our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep homes are non-locked, licensed residential treatment facilities that provide 24-hour staff support.

The Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep is for clients who are beginning to take on more autonomy, but still require prompting and reminders from staff to engage in coping skills and basic activities of daily living.
In our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep level of care, residents can begin their journey of learning new life skills to put them on the path toward greater independence.
Our caring staff supports our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep clients in learning budgeting, grocery shopping, cooking, and more.

Clients in our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep level of care receive treatment and programming services both in their residence and at the BrightQuest Center, where they begin to interact with clients from other homes.
What makes our Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) Prep special is that it truly welcomes our clients into our therapeutic model of healing.

Through our community model, clients can envision life beyond their diagnosis and work on the skills necessary to work toward that goal in a safe, encouraging environment before moving on to our PHP with Housing level of care.
Residential/Partial Hospitalization Program Prep - RTC-2 (PHP-Prep)
Partial Hospitalization Program (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing
Our Residential PHP Prep homes are non-locked, licensed residential treatment facilities that provide 24-hour staff support while clients are in the home.

A bridge between residential and PHP with housing focused on the skills necessary for living without onsite staff support such as medication adherence, healthy self-care, socialization, and organization
PHP with Semi-Independent Housing may be the recommended entry point to BrightQuest for some of our new clients.
Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Housing
Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Semi-Independent Housing is often the recommended entry point to BrightQuest clients.
The PHP with Semi-Independent Housing level of care is often the recommended entry point to BrightQuest for many of our new clients.
IOP with Semi-Independent Housing builds on the skills learned during the PHP with Semi-Independent Housing level of care.
The cornerstones of PHP with Semi-Independent Housing include:

Though no overnight, live-in manager is present at the PHP with Semi-Independent Housing and IOP with Semi-Independent Housing levels of care, BrightQuest staff regularly visits each throughout the day to verify medication compliance, chore completion and to confirm that the home is in good order.

Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient – BrightQuest Treatment Centers
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient Services

Led by a team of skilled professionals, our Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) and Outpatient Services options offer BrightQuest clients an individualized therapeutic structure after completing the BrightQuest Program.
Benefits of Using our IOP and Outpatient Services
Clients in our IOP and Outpatient Program will receive exceptional treatment coordinated to work harmoniously with employment, school or other important commitments in their everyday lives.
For more information about our IOP or Outpatient Services, contact our admission department at 619-466-0547.
Advantages of our IOP and Outpatient Services Program include:

Continued Support: Our IOP and Outpatient Services offerings provide individuals the opportunity to extend their treatment as a means of maintaining the therapeutic improvements made during residential treatment as well as building upon those achievements.
Intensive Outpatient Program (IOP) with Housing
IOP
IOP with Semi-Independent Housing
Our IOP homes are residential homes located in the surrounding community.
This is for clients who have completed our IOP.
Partial Hospitalization (PHP) with Housing and Intensive Outpatient (IOP) with Housing

rawMentions: Whereas hospitalization tends to permit very limited short-range goals, integrative treatment approaches can address the symptoms of a disorder or co-occurring disorders, as well as the many life factors that influence one’s mental health and well-being.
Mood Disorders and Co-Occurring Issues
When you bring your loved one to BrightQuest for mood disorder treatment, they’ll undergo a full evaluation for any and all co-occurring disorders.
Their treatment plan will include extensive and intensive wellness services, targeted at their mood disorders symptoms and any additional co-occurring conditions diagnosed.

rawMentions: 1 family therapy session per week
Additional family therapy sessions

Family therapy sessions are a weekly occurrence in most treatment plans, and these uplifting and transformative meetings will help you gain greater understanding of your loved one’s struggles with complex mental illness.
Intensive psychotherapy in individual, group, and family therapy formats
Family therapy centers on psychoeducation, setting goals, and stabilizing patterns
Family therapy centers on history, communication, and healthy coping strategies
Family therapy concentrates on maintaining skills such as setting and holding limits and boundaries, and open communication.
Family therapy includes exploring family roles and relational dynamics
Family therapy concentrates on ongoing maintenance of skills and adjusted familial roles with minimal clinical prompting and planning for the transition into independent living
